title: TERP Terminology Entry Schema
description: JSON Schema for a single entry in the TERP canonical term map. Every term in term-map.json must validate against this schema.

term.description: The unique identifier key for this term (snake_case, e.g. 'sales_order')

canonical.description: The canonical human-readable name for this term (e.g. 'Sales Order'). This is what must appear in code, APIs, and documentation.

family.description: Which vocabulary family this term belongs to. Must be one of the 5 defined families.

source.description: Authority source mapping for this term

authority.description: The authoritative source that defines this term. 'db_schema' means the Drizzle schema is the single source of truth. 'evan_vocabulary' means Evan's spoken/written preference overrides any technical naming. 'db_schema+evan_vocabulary' means both agree. 'ui_only' means the term exists only in the UI layer (no DB/API equivalent).

db_table.description: The Drizzle schema table where this term lives. Null if no DB equivalent (ui_only terms).

db_column.description: The primary DB column reference for this term. Null if no DB equivalent.

api_field.description: The tRPC/API field name that represents this term. Null if no API equivalent.

ui_label.description: The exact string that appears (or should appear) in the UI for this term.

code_file.description: Optional: path to the primary source file that implements or defines this term (relative to repo root).

deprecated_aliases.description: List of deprecated names for this term that MUST NOT appear in new code. These are scanned by the terminology census and drift audit scripts.

context.description: A concise explanation of what this term means in the TERP system and how to use it correctly. Must be sufficient for a new developer to understand correct usage without additional research.

policy_lock.description: Policy enforcement configuration for this term

locked.description: If true, the deprecated_aliases for this term are actively enforced by the drift audit script and CI. Any new code using a deprecated alias will fail the CI gate.

rule.description: Human-readable statement of the policy rule for this term. Example: 'Never use Vendor in UI. Always use Supplier.'

exceptions.description: Optional list of file patterns where deprecated aliases are still tolerated (for legacy backward-compatibility shims). Format: glob patterns relative to repo root.

condition.description: The condition under which the variant label is used (e.g. 'product category is flower, pre-roll, smalls, shake, or trim')

label.description: The label to use when the condition is met (e.g. 'Farmer')

dynamic_variants.description: Optional: dynamic UI label variants based on context (e.g. Brand vs Farmer based on product category). Only used for terms that have context-dependent labels.

notes.description: Optional: additional notes, migration status, or cross-references.
